title: helianthemum vulgare subsp. chamaecistus
intro: Helianthemum vulgare subsp. chamaecistus, also known as rockrose or sunrose, is a perennial plant species belonging to the Cistaceae family, characterized by its pink to white flowers and woody stems. Traditionally, the plant has been used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and astringent properties. The health benefits of rockrose include its ability to soothe wounds, reduce fever, and alleviate digestive issues, as well as its potential to act as an antiviral and antibacterial agent. Rockrose can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are often used to treat various health conditions, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and potential interactions with other medications.
